name: experiment-lifecycle
description: Run GPU experiments like a researcher — the full lifecycle in six skills. design-experiment: propose with taste, write a DESIGN.md data-collection spec (what reliable, comparable data this produces + what it's designed to inform — not a pre-registered verdict), clear it with the human through verify-claims gates, emit a thin START.md brief + CHECKLIST.md gates, dispatch to a fresh-context executor. run-experiment: the zero-context executor — arm an independent self-wake for autonomous detached runs, acquire compute (gpu-job), provision + drive + collect + close (RESULTS describes the data per the DESIGN spec, cross-family audit, teardown). log-experiment: log a finished experiment, a design-stage pre-registration, an exploration, a dataset, or a note to the research repo as a GATED pull request and merge it — classified by the registry convention (experiment → verify the close-audit; design-stage → verify the design-audit + secret scan; exploration → verify the Status: EXPLORATORY header + secret scan; dataset → verify a sha256 table + an R2 path + secret scan; note → deterministic secret scan), cross-family bot approval satisfies branch protection. log-exploratory: the recipe for a quick, interactive, exploratory analysis that still gets a durable registry-note record — below design-experiment/run-experiment's threshold, landed via log-experiment's note path, with a stated escalation boundary once a number needs to become load-bearing. update-dashboard: the post-close edit loop for ONE experiment's operational dashboard page — live-resolve [recipes.viewer], edit the bespoke per-experiment builder, rebuild + verify the served page, land the source via log-experiment (refuses unless the experiment is closed and a dashboard already exists). update-site: the local-first, researcher-driven editorial visualization loop for the cross-experiment site — resolve the record + verified artifacts, enter the instance's visualization-preview recipe, iterate on the page locally, and only on an explicit publish/ship instruction transition to the configured gated landing path. The two are distinguished by destination (dashboard vs. site), and both are distinct from run-experiment's own close-time viewer publish leg. Instance specifics live in an execution profile; pairs with gpu-job (backend) and verify-claims (gates).
